prompt.text: Sortiere: takes (jetzt) oder took (früher)?

groups.label: Now (takes)
members: It takes me ten minutes to walk to school.
It takes her an hour to cook dinner.
How long does it take you to get dressed?

groups.label: Yesterday (took)
members: It took us two hours to drive home.
It took the class an hour to finish the project.
How long did it take you to read the book?

items.hintDe: takes und does gehören zur Gegenwart, took und did zur Vergangenheit.
items.explainDe: takes/does zeigen die Gegenwart, took/did die Vergangenheit. So erkennst du, in welche Gruppe ein Satz gehört.
items.note: group-sort present vs past; grant: takes, took, minute(s), hour, walk, finish

prompt.text: Sortiere: richtig oder falsch?

members: It takes me twenty minutes to walk to school.

It takes me twenty minutes walk to school.
It taked us an hour to cook dinner.

prompt.text: Du möchtest sagen, dass du den ganzen Flug über gelesen hast. Welcher Satz ist richtig?

answers.text: I was reading during the whole flight.

distractors: I was reading while the whole flight.

I was reading by the flight.
items.hintDe: Vor einem Nomen wie the whole flight steht during, und danach kommt kein Satz.
items.explainDe: during steht vor dem Nomen the whole flight. while bräuchte einen Satz; during + Satz ist falsch; by the flight ergibt keinen Sinn.

distractorPool: I was reading while the whole flight.
I was reading during I was on the plane.
I was reading by the flight.
I was reading while the flight.
I was reading until the whole flight long.

prompt.text: Zwei Dinge passieren gleichzeitig: Mum fährt, du liest. Welcher Satz ist richtig?

answers.text: While Mum was driving, I was reading.

distractors: During Mum was driving, I was reading.
While the drive, I was reading.
During I was reading, Mum was driving.
items.hintDe: Vor einem Satz mit Verb (Mum was driving) steht While, nicht During.
items.explainDe: while steht vor einem Satz mit Verb. During + Satz ist falsch; da the drive ein Nomen ist, müsste dort during stehen.

distractorPool: During Mum was driving, I was reading.
While the drive, I was reading.
During I was reading, Mum was driving.
During the drive, Mum was driving.
While the car, I was reading.

items.note: cp: while + clause vs during errors

prompt.text: Du möchtest sagen: Als sie ankamen, hatte der Film schon begonnen. Welcher Satz ist richtig?

answers.text: By the time they arrived, the film had already started.

distractors: By they arrived, the film had already started.
During they arrived, the film had already started.
By the time of they arrived, the film had already started.
items.hintDe: by the time besteht aus drei Wörtern und danach steht ein Satz (they arrived).
items.explainDe: by the time bleibt vollständig und steht vor einem Satz. By allein, During + Satz und by the time of sind falsch.

distractorPool: By they arrived, the film had already started.
During they arrived, the film had already started.
By the time of they arrived, the film had already started.
Until they arrived, the film had already started.
While they arrived, the film had already started.

prompt.text: We had breakfast. Then we went to school. (Verbinde die Sätze mit before.)

answers.text: We had breakfast before we went to school.

prompt.text: Schreibe einen Satz mit until: Die Kinder warteten lange. Dann kam der Bus.

answers.text: They waited a long time until the bus came.
They waited until the bus came.

items.explainDe: until zeigt den Endpunkt des Wartens: bis der Bus kommt.

prompt.text: Schreibe einen Satz mit by the time: Das Flugzeug landet. Dann ist die Reise vorbei.

answers.text: By the time the plane lands, the journey is over.

prompt.text: Während des Unterrichts darf man nicht essen.

prompt.text: Welcher Satzanfang passt zu welchem Ende?
